title: GRE Verbal Reasoning
description: GRE Verbal: Text Completion, vocabulary in context, and Reading Comprehension across difficulty levels.

questions.prompt: Although the critics found the novel ______, readers embraced its sprawling, unhurried storytelling.
questions.explanation: "Although" signals contrast: critics disliked it while readers embraced it, so the blank must be negative — "tedious."

questions.prompt: Select the word that most nearly means 'to make worse':
questions.explanation: Exacerbate means to make a problem worse. The others all mean to ease or improve.

questions.prompt: The scientist's ______ approach, testing every assumption, slowed the project but ensured reliable results.
questions.explanation: Testing every assumption describes a careful, detail-oriented approach — "meticulous."

questions.prompt: Choose the word most opposite in meaning to 'transient':
questions.explanation: Transient means short-lived; its opposite is "permanent." The others are synonyms.

questions.prompt: Her argument was so ______ that even her opponents conceded its logic.
questions.explanation: If opponents conceded its logic, the argument was clear and convincing — "cogent."

questions.passage: Coral reefs occupy less than one percent of the ocean floor, yet they support roughly a quarter of all marine species. This disproportion has long puzzled ecologists, who note that the surrounding tropical waters are remarkably nutrient-poor. The prevailing explanation is that reefs recycle nutrients with extraordinary efficiency.

choices.text: describe how coral reefs were first discovered
explain an apparent paradox and the leading account of it
argue that reefs should be protected from pollution

questions.explanation: The passage poses a puzzle (high biodiversity despite nutrient-poor waters) and gives the prevailing explanation (efficient nutrient recycling).
Ephemeral means lasting for a very short time.

questions.prompt: The senator's speech was full of ______: vague promises that committed her to nothing in particular.
questions.explanation: "Vague promises that committed her to nothing" defines platitudes — trite, empty statements. The others imply concrete content.

questions.prompt: A 'laconic' reply is best described as:
questions.explanation: Laconic means terse — using few words.

questions.passage: Although solar power was once dismissed as prohibitively expensive, the cost per watt has fallen so steeply that it now rivals fossil fuels in many markets. Critics who focus on intermittency, however, note that the sun does not always shine, and that affordable, large-scale storage remains the key obstacle to relying on solar around the clock.
questions.prompt: The passage implies that the main remaining barrier to wider solar adoption is:

choices.text: the high cost per watt of solar panels

questions.explanation: The passage says cost has fallen to rival fossil fuels, so cost is no longer the barrier; it identifies storage (for intermittency) as "the key obstacle."
